channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Fuel Price Surge Threatens South African Economy and Households
item.description: South Africa is bracing for historic fuel price hikes from 1 April, with petrol expected to rise by more than R5 a litre, diesel by R10, and paraffin by R11. The sharp increases, linked to turmoil in ...

item.title: UNEP warns that global food waste threatens climate goals and food security
item.description: The United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP) has highlighted a staggering global issue: more than one billion tonnes of edible food are wasted each year, representing roughly one-fifth of all food ...

item.title: PM Modi accuses Kerala’s main fronts of mismanagement, says BJP aims for clean governance
item.description: During an election rally in Palakkad ahead of the 2026 Kerala assembly polls, Prime Minister Narendra Modi declared that the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP)-led National Democratic Alliance (NDA) is the ...

item.title: Pakistan offers to host US‑Iran talks as quad ministers push for negotiated end to Gulf conflict
item.description: Pakistan nehnay ko signal diyaa hai ke wah future mein United States aur Iran ke darmiyan seedhi baat‑cheet ki mizaab (host) ban sakta hai, jab Deputy Prime Minister aur Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar ne ...

item.title: Indus River Authority Faces Hurdles Ahead of Crucial Water Management Meeting
item.description: Pakistan's Indus River System Authority (IRSA) is grappling with significant challenges as it prepares for a vital meeting focused on water management. The authority, responsible for coordinating wate...

item.title: PM assures sufficient fuel reserves amid Middle East crisis
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif stated that Pakistan holds adequate petroleum reserves to meet national requirements despite the global oil market disruptions caused by the ongoing US-Israel conflict wi...

item.title: Trump’s Cuba remarks and Cuba’s enduring solidarity: a reflective piece
item.description: The article discusses how US President Donald Trump’s casual rhetoric about “taking Cuba” sparked global conversations, emphasizing Cuba’s historical support for communities under aggression and occup...

item.title: Middle East tensions threaten Pakistan’s remittance and job stability
item.description: Pakistan’s economy is currently navigating a stabilisation phase, but rising instability in the Middle East poses a major threat to two critical economic lifelines: overseas employment and workers’ re...

item.title: Pakistan's Mediation Amid Intensifying US-Iran Tensions
item.description: The article by Maleeha Lodhi discusses the volatile situation following President Donald Trump’s mixed messages on seeking peace with Iran while simultaneously hinting at military escalation. After su...

item.title: Australia cuts fuel tax to ease petrol prices amid Middle East conflict
item.description: Australia's government announced a temporary reduction of the fuel tax as petrol prices soared due to the war in the Middle East. Prime Minister Anthony Albanese, after a meeting with state and territ...

item.title: Pakistan Urges Dialogue After Temporary Afghan Ceasefire Ends
item.description: Following a brief pause in hostilities during Eidul Fitr, Pakistan has resumed Operation Ghazab lil‑Haq, targeting terrorist hideouts and Afghan Taliban sites across the border. The operation, launche...

item.title: Mt Kenya Politics: MP Kiaraho's Death Triggers By-Election Supremacy Battle
item.description: The national dailies in Kenya have been buzzing about the sudden passing of Ol Kalou MP, David Njuguna Kiaraho. This has caused quite a stir, particularly in the Mt Kenya region, as it's likely to tri...

item.title: Moments that shaped history on March 30
item.description: This feature recaps notable historical moments that occurred on March 30 across different years. The timeline begins in 1282 with the War of the Vespers in Sicily, sparked by a violent incident in Pal...

item.title: State Theatre shines at Naledi Theatre Awards, reaffirming its cultural significance
item.description: The South African State Theatre reinforced its standing as a cornerstone of the country’s cultural landscape by delivering a standout showing at the 21st Naledi Theatre Awards. The awards highlighted ...

item.title: Nyeri Road Crash Claims Life of Kenya Forest Officer Returning from Hospital
item.description: A tragic traffic accident along the Meru–Nyahururu Highway in Nyeri County claimed sixteen lives after a lorry ferrying firewood collided with a matatu carrying passengers near Dedan Kimathi Universit...

item.title: Ndovu Wealth Introduces Kibaba Multi-Asset Fund to Help Kenyan Investors Diversify Globally
item.description: Ndovu Wealth, a Kenyan investment platform founded in 2022 by Radhika Bhachu and Rogito Nyageri, has announced the launch of the Kibaba Multi-Asset Special Fund. This collective investment scheme aims...

item.title: Kenya Power cuts electricity theft by 52 per cent through smart meters and better grid connections
item.description: Kenya Power has reported a significant 52 per cent reduction in electricity dumping, commonly known as power theft or illegal connections, following the rollout of improved customer connections across...

item.title: Waste Disposal Practices in Dar es Salaam Bring Short-Term Benefits but Long-Term Health Risks
item.description: In Dar es Salaam, Tanzania’s largest city, local communities have been using market waste to fill gullies and prevent erosion that threatens their homes. Anthropologist Declan Murray calls this practi...

item.title: Toyota Introduces 2026 Land Cruiser Prado with Improved Performance, Hybrid Powertrain, and Advanced Safety Tech
item.description: Toyota has rolled out the 2026 Land Cruiser Prado, which brings back its rugged off-road heritage while embracing modern design and hybrid technology. The new Prado, also known as the LC250, is built ...

item.title: Tanzania equips social welfare officers with smartphones to enhance service delivery
item.description: The Government of Tanzania has taken a major step towards modernising social welfare services by equipping Social Welfare Officers in all 26 mainland regions with smartphones. The initiative, led by M...
